     The Numbers Business Report

Casino Royale (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $167,365,000Details
International Box Office $427,055,216
Worldwide Box Office $594,420,216
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $84,066,502
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,482,296
Total $85,548,798
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After receiving a license to kill, British Secret Service agent James Bond heads to Madagascar, where he uncovers a link to Le Chiffre, a man who finances terrorist organizations. Learning that Le Chiffre plans to raise money in a high-stakes poker game, MI6 sends Bond to play against him, gambling that their newest “00” operative will topple the man’s organization.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$40,833,156 (24.4% of total gross)
Legs:4.10 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:28.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$102,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,434 opening theaters/3,443 max. theaters, 6.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $287,858,388

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 17th, 2006 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: December 7th, 2006 (Wide) (New Zealand)
December 7th, 2007 (Wide) (Australia)
June 10th, 2019 (Special Engagement), released as Casino Royale (Secret Cinema 2019) (United Kingdom)
November 13th, 2020 (Limited) (United Kingdom)
August 12th, 2021 (Wide) (New Zealand)
Video Release: March 13th, 2007 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of violent action, a scene of torture, sexual content and nudity
Running Time: 144 minutes
Franchise: James Bond
Cast, crew, or production detail: Gratuitous Cameos, Slow Motion Scene
Plot point: Gambling, Hostage, Martial Arts, Parkour, Secret Agent, Terrorism, Undercover
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Eon Productions, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After receiving a license to kill, British Secret Service agent James Bond heads to Madagascar, where he uncovers a link to Le Chiffre, a man who finances terrorist organizations. Learning that Le Chiffre plans to raise money in a high-stakes poker game, MI6 sends Bond to play against him, gambling that their newest “00” operative will topple the man’s organization.

Opening Weekend:$40,833,156 (24.4% of total gross)
